rationale: We must exploit our military advantage with precision strikes to degrade Tethys’s command infrastructure and reverse momentum before political erosion undermines our position.
A show of force in contested waters will reinforce our strategic reach and deter further coalition arms shipments to Tethys.
Limited ground penetrations will test enemy defenses, disrupt logistics, and demonstrate unwavering resolve to our domestic base.
Conduct disruptive cyber operations against Tethys’s military logistics networks to amplify the impact of Krasnov’s precision strikes and degrade their battlefield coordination.
Disable key rail and supply nodes in contested regions to hinder Tethys’s ability to reinforce frontlines, complementing our naval deployment by increasing pressure on their operational capacity.
Stage a provocation that exposes Western hypocrisy in supporting Tethys, reversing recent information losses and undermining international legitimacy for continued aid.
Precision economic sanctions on Tethys’s energy and defense sectors will amplify military pressure without triggering broad humanitarian fallout that could erode international tolerance.
Devaluing our currency relative to Tethys’s undermines their import capacity and inflation stability, exploiting their 29% territorial gains as a logistical burden.
Restricting critical raw material exports to Tethys degrades their war machine’s sustainability while aligning with Krasnov’s strikes and Morozov’s sabotage efforts.
Strengthening our frontline positions will exploit Novaris’s logistical strain and degrade their offensive momentum.
Increasing vigilance across contested zones will deter exploitation of weak points while preserving defensive integrity.
Probing enemy movements will provide critical intelligence to anticipate overextensions and prepare countermeasures.
Our forces are vulnerable to infiltration amid high crisis conditions; strengthening internal security will protect operational integrity and support General Bondar’s defensive posture.
With escalating cyber incidents and unclear attribution, safeguarding our critical infrastructure is essential to maintaining military and economic resilience.
Providing verified intelligence to Tethys strengthens alliance cohesion and enhances coordinated defense without escalating offensive actions.

Precision sanctions on key Novaris defense-sector allies will amplify economic strain without triggering broader retaliation, aligning with military and intelligence efforts to exploit their logistical fragility.
Restricting critical dual-use imports to Novaris degrades their war-sustaining production capacity, reinforcing our defensive military posture by weakening long-term offensive reach.
Controlled depreciation of our currency boosts export competitiveness and domestic production, offsetting sanction-related revenue losses and sustaining wartime economic resilience.

Leveraging our strong international support, we should convene a formal multilateral forum under UN auspices to legitimize our defense and institutionalize coalition backing, reinforcing General Bondar’s strategic position.
Building on existing military aid, we can deepen operational coordination with Tethys and allied states to standardize responses and deter further aggression through unified diplomatic posture.

Inviting neutral international observers to monitor our conduct bolsters our credibility, counters Novaris’ propaganda, and aligns with liberal-institutionalist principles while supporting Savchenko’s cyber_defense by safeguarding information integrity.
